title: options: align underwriting implementation with smart-contract specification
description:
The current packages/options/sources/underwriting.move implementation is misaligned with spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. Fix all findings in one coherent change:

1. Underwritten omits the required long_token_id field. Add the minted Long object ID to the event.
2. underwrite_call and underwrite_put currently return Long, seller premium, and fee, allowing callers to retain or redirect assets while supplying buyer and fee_recipient values that only affect event metadata. Each function must enforce the specified transfers: send Long to buyer, premium_total - operational_fee to the seller, and operational_fee to fee_recipient.
3. Underwriting does not enforce the protocol global pause for already-created series. Add the required pause check so call and put underwriting abort while paused.

Design constraint: underwrite_call and underwrite_put MUST NOT be designed as PTB-composable variants returning assets. We need exactly one production implementation of each operation, and each must perform the required transfers internally according to the specification. Remove or consolidate redundant *_and_transfer wrappers so there is only one authoritative call path per option type.
acceptance_criteria: Underwritten includes long_token_id; underwrite_call internally transfers Long to buyer, net premium to seller, and fee to fee_recipient; underwrite_put does the same; neither function returns those assets for PTB composition; only one production implementation exists for call underwriting and one for put underwriting; both abort under the protocol global pause; Move tests cover recipient transfers, event data, fee rules, and pause behavior; sui move test passes.
close_reason: Human stamp as done/completed after local code review

title: Deepen Series by internalizing CollateralPool custody
description:
Why this exists: callers currently have to pass and coordinate Series plus CollateralPool, so they must know the matching invariant and custody storage shape. This keeps the Series Interface shallow around underwriting, exercise, and seller settlement.

What needs to be done: refactor Series custody so CollateralPool is internal to Series as a stored custody Module/struct, not a separate shared object input. Public underwriting, exercise, and settlement Interfaces must no longer take CollateralPool from callers. Series should remain the external Seam and decide lifecycle facts; CollateralPool should be an internal Implementation Module that handles collateral deposits, exercise proceeds, SellerVault payout allocation, accounted balances, payout calculation, overdraw prevention, and dust/excess accounting.

Verification: this addresses the architectural issue only if CollateralPool disappears from caller-facing Interfaces and tests assert custody outcomes through Series actions.
acceptance_criteria: Public underwriting, exercise, and settlement functions do not accept CollateralPool as an argument; Series owns one internal CollateralPool custody field; tests cover call and put underwriting, manual exercise, seller settlement, custody solvency, overdraw failures, and dust/excess accounting through the Series Interface; spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md describes CollateralPool as internal Series custody and no longer requires a separate shared CollateralPool object or collateral pool id event field.
close_reason: Internalized CollateralPool as UID-bearing Series custody; removed pool from caller APIs/events; added Series custody, dust, solvency, and overdraw coverage.

title: Fix underwriting max operational fee to use market-configured basis points
description: Current underwriting uses max_operational_fee as an optional absolute coin amount, which varies from OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. Fix the implementation to use a market-configured maximum fee in basis points and reject underwriting when operational_fee exceeds the configured basis-points cap over premium_total.
close_reason: Completed

title: options: implement market uniqueness and per-market pause
description: Implement the latest Market requirements from spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. Market creation must reject duplicates with the same OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combination. Admin pause authority must apply per market, and paused-market restrictions must be enforced for series creation and underwriting. The protocol may additionally expose a helper that pauses all markets in one transaction, but that helper is optional.
acceptance_criteria: Creating a duplicate OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin market aborts; distinct combinations remain creatable, including different wrapped BaseCoin types; an admin can pause and unpause an individual market; pausing one market does not pause another; series creation and underwriting abort for a paused market; existing allowed operations retain the specification's pause behavior; Move tests cover duplicate rejection and market-isolated pause behavior; sui move test passes.

title: spec: define per-market pause and duplicate market prevention
description: Update spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md to require per-market admin pause, optionally allow pausing all markets in one transaction, and require duplicate-market rejection for identical OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combinations.
acceptance_criteria: Market creation rejects duplicate OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combinations; pause is explicitly per-market and admin controlled; protocol-wide batch pause is explicitly optional.
close_reason: Updated the specification with per-market pause semantics, optional all-market pause batching, and duplicate market rejection.

title: Review codebase architecture depth
description: Surface architectural friction and deepening opportunities in a temp HTML report, using project domain vocabulary and architecture vocabulary.
close_reason: Generated temp architecture review report and opened it for review

title: Add options full lifecycle smoke test
description: Add a single Move test covering market creation, series creation, seller underwriting, ITM expiry finalization, buyer exercise, seller settlement, and series closure in one flow.
close_reason: Added full options call lifecycle smoke test and verified with sui move test.

title: Add signed buyer order underwriting path
description: The options package currently underwrites by accepting premium/collateral inputs directly and does not expose order structs or signature verification. Add a buyer order model and signature-verified underwriting entrypoint if off-chain buyer orders are required.

title: Define operator role for options lifecycle
description: The options package currently has AdminCap for market creation and expiry price creation, while series creation and seller settlement are public permissionless calls. Define and implement a distinct operator role if market/series/finalization/settlement should be operator-gated.

title: Validate options end-to-end lifecycle
description: Manually validate or add a single Move spec test covering admin/operator market and series creation, seller underwriting with buyer order signature, expiry finalization ITM, buyer exercise, and operator settlement for the options package.
close_reason: Validated options package lifecycle with sui move test; full suite passed, with signed order and operator-role gaps filed separately.

title: Address expiry adapter review comments
description: Review feedback requests Pyth canonical oracle validation, removal of unnecessary payload hash Series storage, and a clearer single-series finalization helper shape.
close_reason: Addressed expiry adapter review comments

title: Document permissioned Pyth expiry finalization MVP
description: Update the options smart contract spec to define the MVP expiry price finalization design: permissioned Pyth unverifiable adapter, ExpiryPrice hot potato, and fixed-arity batch finalization helpers.
close_reason: Updated OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md with permissioned Pyth unverifiable expiry finalization, ExpiryPrice hot potato, and fixed-arity batch helpers.

title: Migrate Long quantity to Balance-backed accounting
description: OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md requires Long token quantity as Sui::Balance. Evaluate and implement migrating Long.quantity from u64 to Sui::Balance if it makes the Long token module design better. If Balance worsens the Long token design, defer this task and document the explanation for why Long.quantity as Sui::Balance is worse than the current u64 design.
notes: Deferred by automation on 2026-06-16: OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md requires Long.quantity as Sui::Balance, but the current design mints Long claim quantity while retaining actual BaseCoin/QuoteCoin collateral in the Series CollateralPool. A Sui Balance<T> is an actual coin-balance resource, so making Long.quantity a Balance<BaseCoin> needs a clarified accounting design: either Long holds real collateral balance, which conflicts with pool custody, or the protocol creates synthetic balances, which risks coin supply/resource semantics. Clarify whether the spec means Balance-backed real coin custody, a custom accounting balance type, or keep u64 claim quantity.
close_reason: Rejected Balance-backed Long quantity. Long.quantity is an option claim amount, while sui::balance::Balance<T> represents real coin supply/resource accounting. Using Balance for Long would imply coin/token supply semantics or collateral custody on the Long object, which conflicts with the current design where BaseCoin/QuoteCoin collateral stays in the Series CollateralPool. Keep Long.quantity as u64 unless Longs are intentionally redesigned as separate fungible Sui assets.

title: Extract Long token module
description: Move Long token object mechanics into a dedicated Move module while keeping pricing, exercise windows, claim pools, and seller accounting in core options modules.
close_reason: Completed Long token module extraction

title: Enforce 8 hour minimum time to expiry for options
description: Update the options spec and Move implementation so series creation and underwriting are rejected when expiry is within 8 hours.
close_reason: Implemented 8 hour minimum time-to-expiry rule in spec and series creation tests/code.

title: Parameterize market test fixture
description: Update create_market_fixture to accept market creation arguments and add coverage that multiple markets with distinct base/quote/collateral combinations can be created.
close_reason: Implemented parameterized market fixture and added multiple-market creation coverage.

title: Remove SPEC contract contradictions
description: Remove parts of SPEC.md that conflict with spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md so the product spec delegates canonical on-chain behavior to the smart-contract spec.
close_reason: Removed SPEC.md content that contradicted OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md and delegated canonical on-chain mechanics to the smart-contract spec.

title: Recovery after settlement
description:
## What to build

Allow admin recovery of excess balances and rounding dust after manual, fully exercised, ATM, or OTM settlement paths without touching active obligations.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Recovery is limited to balances not reserved for active, exercisable, or unsettled series obligations.
- [ ] Rounding dust is recoverable only after the configured conservative recovery delay.
- [ ] Recovery emits the required admin recovery event with asset, amount, recipient, and reason code.

## Blocked by

- Manual exercise and seller settlement
notes: Deferred by automation: acceptance requires rounding dust recovery only after the configured conservative recovery delay, but neither spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md nor the issue defines the delay duration or the configuration surface. Please clarify whether this should be a package constant, market-level setting, series-level setting, and the exact duration. Also confirm reason-code values for AdminRecovered.

title: Manual exercise and seller settlement
description:
## What to build

Implement holder-initiated manual physical exercise and permissionless seller settlement for no-exercise and fully exercised series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Manual exercise consumes the whole matching long token, enforces the finalized ITM exercise window, validates payment, and transfers the correct physical settlement asset.
- [ ] Exercise accounting records quantities and proceeds for both calls and puts without overdrawing the collateral pool.
- [ ] Permissionless batched seller settlement pays sellers directly for ATM/OTM and fully exercised ITM outcomes, closes settled vault records, and closes the series when complete.

## Blocked by

- Call and put underwriting
- Price finalization and phase derivation
close_reason: Implemented manual exercise and seller settlement with tests

title: Price finalization and phase derivation
description:
## What to build

Finalize immutable expiry prices through the permissioned admin/operator price finalization adapter and expose deterministic phase and moneyness behavior for option series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Price finalization is available after expiry through the admin/operator adapter, validates oracle identity/feed id/publish time/positive price, and stores the price immutably.
- [ ] Batch finalization only accepts series from the same market.
- [ ] Derived phase and ITM/ATM/OTM behavior matches the spec for calls, puts, manual exercise, exception window, partial settlement, and full settlement.

## Blocked by

- Expiry price finalization adapter
- Series creation with collateral pool
notes: Deferred by automation on 2026-06-16: implementation and tests now cover after-expiry finalization, immutable price storage, oracle/feed/publish/positive price checks, same-market batch rejection, and call/put phase boundaries. Resolution on 2026-06-16: preserve the permissioned admin/operator adapter described in spec/OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md; acceptance was updated from permissionless finalization to admin/operator adapter finalization.
close_reason: Completed: implementation and tests satisfy clarified admin-cap price finalization and phase derivation acceptance criteria.

title: Call and put underwriting
description:
## What to build

Support fully collateralized call and put underwriting that creates transferable long option tokens, records seller short obligations, and handles premium and fee settlement.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Call underwriting deposits BaseCoin collateral and put underwriting deposits QuoteCoin collateral using solvency-favoring strike payment rounding.
- [ ] Underwriting mints the correct long token to the buyer, updates one seller vault record per seller/series, and increases series short quantity.
- [ ] Premium, operational fee, fee recipient, and optional max-fee checks are enforced atomically.
- [ ] Long tokens support split and join only for identical option class and series fields.

## Blocked by

- Series creation with collateral pool
close_reason: Implemented fully collateralized call/put underwriting with long tokens, seller vault accounting, premium/fee handling, and tests.

title: Market admin and pause lifecycle
description:
## What to build

Implement the shared Market lifecycle with admin authority, supported asset identity, and global pause behavior for the options protocol.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Markets store the OracleBase / QuoteCoin / BaseCoin combination, oracle metadata, decimals, strike scale, supported coin types, admin authority, and pause state.
- [ ] Unsupported coin types are rejected by market-scoped operations introduced in this slice.
- [ ] Pause and unpause require admin authority and emit the required events.

## Blocked by

- Sui Move project lean setup
close_reason: Implemented shared market lifecycle with admin capability, coin-type guards, pause/unpause events, and TDD coverage.

title: Series creation with collateral pool
description:
## What to build

Allow anyone to create a valid option series for an existing market, with exactly one isolated collateral pool for that series.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Series creation rejects invalid option type, zero strike, past expiry, unsupported market coin types, and duplicate market/type/strike/expiry tuples.
- [ ] Each created series references exactly one collateral pool for that series and emits series creation data.
- [ ] Series state and timing fields support the spec's derived expiry phases without adding extra stored phase states.

## Blocked by

- Market admin and pause lifecycle
close_reason: Implemented series creation with collateral pool and TDD coverage

title: Expiry price finalization adapter
description:
## What to build

Decide and encode the contract-facing expiry price finalization adapter shape for permissionless option series finalization after expiry.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The accepted oracle source, proof/payload shape, and validation responsibilities are recorded clearly enough for implementation.
- [ ] The adapter design supports market oracle name/feed id checks, positive price checks, publish time at or after expiry, and batch finalization by market.
- [ ] The design leaves exercise blocked when no valid bounded expiry price can be finalized.

## Blocked by

- Sui Move project lean setup
notes:
Deferred for 36h: needs human/product decision before implementation. Missing requirements: accepted oracle source, proof/payload shape, and exact validation responsibility boundary for the expiry price adapter.
The option smart contract specification has been updated to address finalization requirements issues. Take anohter look at the task and the spec, update this task if the spec now answers all the questions and requiments are clear, and if the requirement, spec, task is good to be implemented then work on it.
close_reason: Implemented Pyth unverifiable expiry price adapter and fixed-arity series finalization with tests

title: Sui Move project lean setup
description:
## What to build

Create the lean Sui Move package foundation for the options smart contract work so later slices can add production behavior without reworking project structure.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The repository has a minimal Sui Move package configured for the options protocol.
- [ ] The package builds with the chosen Sui toolchain and contains only scaffold-level modules/tests needed by follow-on slices.
- [ ] The package metadata or local docs make the intended build/test command clear for AFK agents.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
notes: Implemented lean packages/options Sui Move scaffold with Move.toml package name options_trading_protocol, package_info source module, package_info unit test, Move.lock, and README build/test commands. Final validation passed: sui move test and sui move build both complete successfully from packages/options using Sui 1.72.1.
close_reason: Completed lean Sui Move package scaffold; sui move test and sui move build pass for packages/options.

title: Configure max operational fee per options contract spec
description: Add mechanics to configure and enforce max_operational_fee according to OPTIONS_SMART_CONTRACT.md. The spec allows a market admin-configured maximum fee basis points; if configured, underwriting must reject fees above that maximum.

title: Deduplicate underwriting cutoff wording in options spec
description: Define the minimum underwriting time to expiry once in the options smart contract spec and reference it from series creation and underwriting requirements.
close_reason: Defined minimum underwriting time to expiry once and referenced it from series creation and underwriting requirements.

title: Recovery after partial ITM settlement
description:
## What to build

Allow admin recovery after partial ITM settlement where exercised and unexercised obligations were paid through mixed seller settlement.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Recovery accounts for mixed exercise proceeds and returned collateral before identifying excess balances.
- [ ] Recovery cannot withdraw anything reserved for unsettled seller vaults or active accounting obligations.
- [ ] Dust and excess recovery remains delayed, evented, and checked against collateral pool accounting.

## Blocked by

- Partial ITM settlement after missed exception

title: Recovery for exercise-by-exception and claim-poll settlement path
description:
## What to build

Allow admin recovery for series settled through exercise-by-exception while preserving all claim pool balances owed to late long holders.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Recovery can clean up excess or dust in old series/collateral objects after claim-pool settlement is complete.
- [ ] Recovery cannot withdraw claim-pool balances reserved for unclaimed matching long tokens.
- [ ] Closed or depleted claim-pool cleanup is supported where storage can be safely reclaimed.

## Blocked by

- Exercise-by-exception and claim pools

title: Partial ITM settlement after missed exception
description:
## What to build

Settle ITM series after the exception window when some longs were manually exercised but exercise-by-exception did not complete.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] After the exception window, remaining unexercised ITM longs are no longer exercisable or claimable when exercise-by-exception did not complete.
- [ ] Seller settlement pays the exercised portion from exercise proceeds and returns original collateral for the unexercised portion.
- [ ] Settlement remains permissionless, batched, pro-rata by seller short quantity, and dust-safe.

## Blocked by

- Manual exercise and seller settlement
- Exercise-by-exception and claim pools

title: Exercise-by-exception and claim pools
description:
## What to build

Implement permissionless exercise-by-exception for remaining ITM longs and create claim pools that late holders can redeem against by burning matching long tokens.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] During the exception window, anyone can settle the remaining unexercised ITM quantity for calls and puts when required seller payment and minimum net claim constraints are satisfied.
- [ ] Net proceeds for late holders are deposited into a claim pool only when non-zero, and the operation records exercise-by-exception accounting.
- [ ] Claim pool redemption burns a matching long token, pays a solvency-rounded pro-rata share, handles final rounding dust safely, and does not require loading old series or seller vault records.

## Blocked by

- Price finalization and phase derivation
- Flash-loan exercise composability

title: Flash-loan exercise composability
description:
## What to build

Make exercise paths composable inside Sui PTBs so callers can combine exercise proceeds with external flash-loan, swap, and repayment routes atomically.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Core exercise functions expose PTB-friendly return values or wrappers that allow proceeds to be routed before final transfer.
- [ ] The options contract does not hard-code any lending protocol, DEX, swap route, or provider-specific dependency.
- [ ] Tests or examples demonstrate that borrow/exercise/repay-style composition can be expressed atomically around the core exercise path.

## Blocked by

- Manual exercise and seller settlement
